search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae headache adults telehealth red flags emergency evaluation
site:nice.org.uk CG150 headache diary reassessment referral follow-up 2025
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic migraine, tension-type, cluster, or medication-overuse diagnosis
automatic imaging, medicine, withdrawal, prophylaxis, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Headache classification requires the exact clinical pattern and clinician assessment.
No test or treatment is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People over 12 under CG150 and adults under DHA telehealth guidance; younger children, pregnancy, postpartum, trauma, immunocompromise, malignancy, acute neurological deficit, and severe systemic illness require tailored pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: Direct DHA adult telehealth evidence supplemented by NICE guidance for people over 12; UAE emergency, imaging, prescribing, and referral pathways remain locally governed.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact headache-feature section supports interval location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medicine-overuse context without assigning headache type.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports headache location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medication-overuse review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA adult red-flag section supports clinician-assessed systemic, neurological, onset, associated-feature, and pattern-change concern plus available examination without asserting absence.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Provides systemic, neurologic, onset, associated-feature, and pattern-change red-flag domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact diary and reassessment section supports recording existing diary, frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, results, clinician discussion, referral status, and follow-up without generating an investigation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording headache frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, and clinician-led further investigation or referral when red flags develop.

unresolved_source_gaps: Current headache pattern, observations, neurological or eye examination, pregnancy, cause, diagnosis, diary content, medicine, test, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
